title: pod crashes running apt update
body:
### Issue Description

I noticed this issue just today when I was attempting to update update packages within some pods. The symptom affects my Debian and Ubuntu pods, not my Fedora pods. I can run apt update just fine, but when I run apt upgrade -y, within a few seconds, right in the middle of the update of packages, the pod crashes and returns me to the host. If I run podman logs, the only error is: "Error: An error occurred".

Any other running pods remain running. Only the pod I was updating packages in at that moment crashes. I found this symptom afflicting my existing pods and new pods. I created a pod based on Debian 11 (all my existing pods are 12), and it also experienced the symptom.

Figure 1: test1 pod is running and preparing to install inkscape packages

![2024-03-28_20-26](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/fdb189f8-5b62-4c0b-8c5e-985db1053962)

Figure 2: test1 pod is no longer running (crashed)
![2024-03-28_20-26_1](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/f8d95230-48a3-42b8-9a4e-daea5b2a1435)

Figure 3: Error message
![2024-03-28_20-26_2](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/5ee57e6f-917e-444a-b5f8-ea28a40a8d9f)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a brand new pod (I use distrobox, but the symptom happens even when updating packages through podman desktop)
_distrobox create --name test1 --image debian:11 --home /home/jwilmoth/distroboxhomes/test1_
2. Enter your pod
_distrobox enter test1   (or podman desktop > terminal)_
3. Wait for it to initialize
4. sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
_If there are no updates, then go ahead and install something, anything. If the error isn't exhibited, try again. I have found the symptom occuring on demand during an install or update of anywhere between 5 and 10 packages._


### Describe the results you received

The pod crashes in the middle of the update, and after starting it up again and connecting, I have to run dpkg --configure -a before I can retry. Often it crashes again on the retry, depending on how much needed to be done.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the pod to remain running like it always has even during an installation or update of one or more packages.

### podman info output

comments.body: do you have a reproducer using only podman?  or just podman restful api? otherwise, i am wondering if this better suited to be filed against podman-desktop.

I'm not sure what you're asking, but it sounds like eliminating podman-desktop. I uninstalled it just now and re-tested. The symptom remains.

Figure 1: distrobox enter test1, followed by sudo apt install openttd
![2024-03-29_17-40](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/ad3ff2ac-6f3d-4d96-94ef-3d1af37f7c9f)

Figure 2: once pressing 'y', installation starts and barely gets off the ground before container crashes and returns to host
![2024-03-29_17-40_1](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/3b3ed64e-d534-45c1-b55a-6504b56cb46e)

Figure 3: podman logs test1 shows same error message
![2024-03-29_17-41](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/26101661/571effb6-8d98-4217-81d9-9817dd098d0d)
im not asking you to uninstall podman-desktop, just take it out of the equation.  using only the podman cli, can you give us a reproducer for your problem ?

Are we speaking the same language? I said "eliminating podman-desktop" and you said "take it out of the equation." They are the same thing practically-speaking. I only installed podman-desktop recently and don't really use it, so I had no issues doing that. Like I said, I use distrobox, not podman directly and not podman-desktop.

I just re-tested again now, and I am not getting the symptom anymore. I updated three containers, even simultaneously, without any issues whatsoever. Super odd. I have no idea why this symptom would have appeared and then disappeared, without any rhyme or reason. Any closing thoughts before we close out this issue?
I would just say reopen if it comes back.
